__purpose__: Ground-truth normalized output for the Bragg agreement — derived from BOTH the DocuSign Navigator metadata export AND the signed PDF. Tests compare the actual normalizer output against these expectations; gaps become XFAIL-ed or a documented schema finding.
__pdf_derived_fields__: agreement_id_number (QUO-12507-F1F2D9-V2025 appears in the rendered agreement trail, not the metadata header)
freight_terms (DAP Garden Grove, CA — only in PDF body)
min_order_quantity (2,000,000 — only in PDF Section regarding POs)
payment_term_discount (1% 10 — PDF says '1% 10 / Net 45 Days'; metadata truncates to '45 days')
alternate_envelope_id (A535A3EE-7BBA-8E79-81DC-09A99ECC3D95 — only in PDF signature trail)

terms_minimum_signals.note: metadata-only view truncates the 1% 10 discount — PDF has the full term
SCHEMA GAP: this is a single hard number; current agreement_terms is string-typed so it works, but reporting will have to cast at query time.

pricing_minimum_signals.location_note: Garden Grove, CA (line_1_location tab — NOT a model field today)

bc_matching_expectations.scenario: Bragg has potentially two BC customer codes (managed by Greg and Susan separately).

expected_behaviour_current_code.description: The current matcher picks the single top-scoring candidate, emits one auto_confirmed or proposed link, and SILENTLY drops the other. No ambiguous-match signal is raised.

expected_behaviour_after_gap_fix.description: When two or more candidates score within X of each other (e.g. both >= propose_threshold), the matcher should emit BOTH as proposed links AND open a `party_unmatched` exception with severity=high and details.ambiguous=true.

known_gaps_at_phase_3_2b: Navigator AI Metadata Export shape is not a Connect SIM payload — the current normalizer cannot read the xlsx/JSON row directly.
Agreement has TWO identifiers that belong on the schema: the Navigator UUID (Agreement Id) and the internal agreement number (QUO-...). Only envelope_id is modeled today.

Some fields live only in the PDF body, not the metadata (freight, MOQ, total_commitment, tooling, annual forecasts, 1% 10 discount). These require either (a) DocuSign template custom-field additions or (b) a PDF extraction pass.
Pricing rows contain a location per line; `AgreementPricing` has no `location` field.
Two envelope IDs appear in the PDF; only one fits on the schema today.
Matcher has no ambiguous-match signal — two equally-scoring BC candidates silently collapse to one.
